summary: Gruber notes that Microsoft's MSN Music help page regarding iPod compatibility has been rewritten again, this time removing overt propaganda. The updated page now straightforwardly states that iPod doesn't support WMA format and directs iPod owners to send feedback to Apple asking them to add Windows Media support. Gruber finds this framing notable, as Microsoft positions Apple as the obstacle to interoperability. The brief post is a follow-up to his earlier piece on the topic.
key_points: Microsoft rewrote its MSN Music iPod help page to remove overtly propagandistic language
The new page frames Apple as the party responsible for iPod owners' inability to play MSN Music purchases
Microsoft directs users to pressure Apple to add WMA support rather than acknowledging its own format lock-in strategy
money_quotes: The MSN Music help page regarding iPod support has been rewritten yet again, this time, sans propaganda.
key_insight: Microsoft's revised MSN Music page dropped the overt propaganda but still cleverly framed Apple as the obstacle to interoperability, deflecting attention from its own proprietary format strategy.
